rulings.Case Title: SF URBAN FOREST COALITION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A CHARTER CITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Re: Petition For Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2018, Line 10, Hearing Re: Petition For Writ Of Mandamus. Petitioner SF Urban Forest Coalition's petition for writ of mandate is denied as to all three causes of action alleged by SFUFC. The portions of SFUFC's first and second causes of action seeking declaratory relief that the San Francisco County Transportation Authority is subject to the San Francisco Sunshine Ordinance lack merit. The SFUFC is an agency of the State of California and thus is exempt from a local ordinance such as the Sunshine Ordinance. Moreover, the language of the Sunshine Ordinance makes clear that the SFTCA is not covered by that Ordinance. SFUFC's argument that the Bay Area Bill provided only "the idea of possibly creating a county transportation agency ¿ and the framework" for such an agency is unpersuasive and not supported by either the text of the Bay Area Bill or analogous case law. Except for the portions of the first and second causes of action in SFUFC's petition seeking declaratory relief that the SFTCA is subject the Sunshine Ordinance, all of SFUFC's claims are moot because SFUFC has acknowledged that it has received all the documents that it requested in its petition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for SFCTA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ISIDRO MIRANDA VS. ANDERSON ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Penalties Sought As Part Of A Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ISIDRO MIRANDA'S Motion To Approve Penalties Sought As Part Of A Settlement. Plaintiff Isidro Miranda's motion to approve settlement of his PAGA claims is denied without prejudice to Mr. Miranda addressing the deficiencies in the moving papers in a re-filed motion, if he can do so in good faith. The moving papers fail to provide a sufficient quantitative or qualitative analysis of any of the asserted violations of the Labor Code which form the basis of Mr. Miranda's PAGA claims. Nor do the moving papers provide a sufficient explanation why Mr. Miranda is receiving any payment, much less $30,000, for his "individual claims" when he did not allege any individual claims and when he is also receiving a "representative incentive payment." The disproportionate amount of money proposed to be paid to Mr. Miranda suggests that he may not be a proper representative plaintiff for either the absent aggrieved parties or the LWDA. Nor do the moving papers provide a sufficient explanation why $5,000 and not some greater amount is proposed to be paid to the LWDA. Lastly, the moving papers fail to provide any data from which the court can calculate a lodestar as a cross-check on the attorney's fees sought by Mr. Miranda's counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Miranda is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE RUDEN VS. C.R. BARD, INC., A NEW JERSEY CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Answers From Dr. William T. Kuo And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 8, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF WAYNE RUDEN's Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Answers From Dr. William T. Kuo And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties to address whether over the physician's objection a treating physician may be compelled to provide expert testimony relating to topics other than percipient involvement in the patient's medical care and treatment. For each question for which a response is sought to be compelled, the moving party shall address the connection between the topic of the question and medical care the witness provided to the plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CECILIA CONTRERAS ET AL VS. CALVIN COOLEDGE WILEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS CECILIA CONTRERAS, JUAN PELAYO'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Plaintiffs Cecilia Contreras and Juan Pelayo's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings. Defendant Calvin Wiley's argument that he will be legally prejudiced by the granting of the motion lacks merit. Because no trial date has been set, Mr. Wiley has ample time to respond to the new allegations. Plaintiffs must file their proposed first amended complaint no later than May 15, 2018. It will not be deemed filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN TOWERS LLC, VS. DIABLO COMMUNICATIONS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANTS RICHARD SPIGHT, DIABLO COMMERCIAL PROPERTIES LLC, MICHAEL SPIGHT, TYRIS CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Hearing required. At the hearing the court will ask counsel their views whether this case should be stayed pending the conclusion of the Sacramento litigation. The court is tentatively of the view that a stay of this case pending the conclusion of the Sacramento litigation furthers judicial economy, avoids the possibility of inconsistent rulings, and eliminates the parties having to litigate this case while the outcome of the Sacramento litigation, which heavily affects both the claims and the damages sought by plaintiff American Towers LLC, is uncertain. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARTER ASSET MANAGMENT FUND, L.P. VS. LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANTS LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC., LATIN COLLEGE PREPARATORY CHARTER SCHOOL, INC.'S Motion To Quash Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction. Defendants Latin Grammar School, Inc. and Latin College Preparatory Charter School, Inc.'s motion to quash is granted as to both defendants. Plaintiff Charter Asset Management Fund, L.P. has not shown that either Christopher Clemons or Erica Anthony had actual or ostensible authority to bind LGS and LCP when they signed the factoring agreements. LGS and LCP did not retain either Mr. Clemons or Ms. Anthony with regard to the factoring agreements nor did they give them authority to enter into those agreements. Mr. Clemons admitted that he lacked authority to enter into the factoring agreements on behalf of LGS and LCP. While ostensible authority is a closer call than actual authority, ostensible authority does not apply when the party asserting ostensible authority acted negligently with regard to the persons claimed to have ostensible authority. (Kaplan v. Coldwell Banker Residential Affiliates, Inc. (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 741, 747.) CAM's failure to determine, much less require, adherence to its own requirement that all members of the boards of LGS and LCP sign resolutions stating that the signatories were authorized to enter into the factoring agreements constitutes negligence precluding a finding of ostensible authority. County First Nat. Bank of Santa Cruz v. Coast Dairies & Land Co. (1941) 46 Cal.App.2d 355 is readily distinguishable because in that case the lender did not impose a requirement that the board of the borrower approve the loan. Absent actual or ostensible authority by Mr. Clemons and Ms. Anthony, there is no basis for this court to assert jurisdiction over LGS and LCP. Neither LGS nor LCP are subject to the specific jurisdiction because neither of them took any actions directed toward California. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for LGS and LCP is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DEPARTMENT OF FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO - DEPARTMENT OF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to both causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Department of Fair Employment and Housing on behalf of real party in interest Tanesh Nutall is overruled as to the first cause of action for violation of the Unruh Act and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for violation of Government Code 11135(a). Liberally construed, the complaint sufficiently alleges facts that show that, at the time of the alleged discrimination against Ms. Nutall, the City was conducting itself as a "business establishment" within the meaning of the Unruh Act. Even with a liberal construction, the complaint does not sufficiently allege facts that show that, at the time of the alleged discrimination against Ms. Nutall, the City was engaged in a state-funded "program or activity." Because it is reasonably possible that DFEH can allege such facts, if it can do so in good faith, it is given leave to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for DFEH is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN MCILROY VS. KIXEYE INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kixeye Inc.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action Pending Arbitration.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2018, Line 14, Defendant Kixeye Inc.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action Pending Arbitration. Defendant Kixeye Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and to stay action pending arbitration is granted in its entirety. All claims alleged by plaintiff Steven McIlroy must be resolved by arbitration per the parties' agreement and this case is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. Assuming without deciding that the arbitration agreement is procedurally unconscionable, it is nonetheless enforceable because it is not substantively unconscionable. The attorneys' fees and costs provision is not substantively unconscionable because it bars the arbitrator from making any fees and costs award that is prohibited by law. The venue provision is not substantively unconscionable because Santa Clara County is not very far from San Francisco. Nor has Mr. McIlroy shown that the arbitration agreement unreasonably limits discovery. Notwithstanding the absence of any specific reference to the Federal Arbitration Act, the FAA applies to this case, at least to the extent that the FAA does not differ from the California Arbitration Act, because the parties' agreement "involves interstate commerce" as that phrase is used in FAA case law. Per Saheli v. White Memorial Medical Center (2018) 21 Cal. App. 5th 308, the reference to "California law" in the arbitration agreement and in paragraph 13 of the parties' agreement refers to California law not preempted by the FAA. Because the FAA preempts Labor Code 229, that section does not apply to the parties' agreement and all of Mr. McIlroy's wage claims are arbitrable, notwithstanding section 229. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Kixeye is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and also complies with CCP 437c(g) and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GARRETT CALLEN VS. MOTIONLOFT INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings, Or In The Alternative, Request For Statement Of Decision
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT MOTIONLOFT INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings, Or In The Alternative, Request For Statement Of Decision. Defendant Motionloft, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is granted in its entirety. All claims alleged by plaintiff Garrett Callen must be resolved by arbitration per the parties' agreement and this case is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. Assuming without deciding that the arbitration agreement is procedurally unconscionable, it is nonetheless enforceable because it is not substantively unconscionable. The attorneys' fees and costs provision is not substantively unconscionable because it bars the arbitrator from making any fees and costs award that is prohibited by law. The venue provision is not substantively unconscionable because Santa Clara County is not very far from San Francisco and Motionloft stipulates to venue in San Francisco. Notwithstanding the absence of any specific reference to the Federal Arbitration Act, the FAA applies to this case, at least to the extent that the FAA does not differ from the California Arbitration Act, because the parties' agreement "involves interstate commerce" as that phrase is used in FAA case law. Per Saheli v. White Memorial Medical Center (2018) 21 Cal. App. 5th 308, the reference to "California law" in the arbitration agreement and in paragraph 13 of the parties' agreement refers to California law not preempted by the FAA. Because the FAA preempts Labor Code 229, that section does not apply to the parties' agreement and all of Mr. Callen's wage claims are arbitrable, notwithstanding section 229. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Motionloft is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and also complies with CCP 437c(g) and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
